activities.markdown:
# Note Groupings

Groupings are repeated patterns of notes that don't divide evenly into 4/4, creating rhythmic interest.

**Common groupings over 4/4:**
- **3-note groupings** (RLK repeating): creates a 3-against-4 polyrhythm
- **4-note groupings** (RLRL): aligns with the beat, straightforward
- **6-note groupings** (RLRLRL): two per beat, creates driving sextuplet feel

**3-note grouping example:**
RLK RLK RLK RLK | RLK RLK RLK R (then resolve)

The pattern takes 12 16th notes to complete — exactly 3 beats.

activities.question: How many 16th notes does a 3-note grouping take to come back to the original hand?
activities.explanation: A 3-note grouping (RLK) repeats: at 3, 6, 9, 12 notes — but in 4/4 with 16 sixteenth notes per measure, it takes 12 sixteenths (3 beats) to return to the right hand on the downbeat.
